summary: On-require beacon exfiltrates installer hostname to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: When @onum-releases/auth is required, index.js reads os.hostname() and issues an HTTPS GET to 'auth.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com/auth'. The installer's hostname is embedded as a subdomain label, leaking host identity via DNS resolution and TLS SNI to a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) instance — infrastructure used to capture out-of-band data during security testing. Beaconing fires on every require() with no opt-in, no documented purpose tied to the package's advertised 'auth' functionality, and no benign explanation. The package self-describes as a PoC beacon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
